subject: [SDC] Update SDC to 1.9.3
commit_message:
[SDC] Update SDC to 1.9.3

Updating SDC images for Istanbul M4.

Signed-off-by: MichaelMorris <michael.morris@est.tech>
Issue-ID: SDC-3694
Change-Id: Ifbba120ef5fd1284a28703260169ab44b6a2bc58

most (if not all) tests using SDC are failing 😞

Patch Set 3:

I believe the /sdc/v1/catalog/resources?resourceType=VF API is good. I suspect the reason it is returning no VFs is due to an issue in importing the VSPs. We have identified an issue in the import and will push a new patchset with dockers where that issue is addressed.

The test cases that are failing are failing in SO rather than SDC, its difficult to see a connection to the changes in SDC. Looking at gating results from other reviews, the same failures can be seen, e.g. : https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//119248-16/index.html, and they passed on a re-run of the tests with no changes. 
Are these failures just down to instability in the tests?

Patch Set 5:

it is wiser to retry

the healthcheck healthdist test is FAIL, which is very unusual
This test is dealing with SDC
the error is when the SDC distributes the model (error from the SO)
https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//124233-5/healthdist/healthdist/report.html

As mentioned by Michael, the errors in smoke tests are different, all the onboarding SDC part seems OK, errors occur on instantiation (4 are ok, 3 are failing)
so it looks like a SO init issue but it is better to check, as mentioned the daily are pretty stable
https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/daily/onap-master-daily-dell/2021-09/23_07-11/

note healthdist is a legacy robot

Patch Set 7:

Gating results seem OK
1) healthcheck => only Holmes issue, affecting all gates so to be ignored
2) smoke
- basic_vm error on SO instantiation, SDC part looks fine, error on SO/openstack as no host was found (heat error) https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//124233-7/basic_vm/basic_vm/pythonsdk.debug.log, strange because other tests dealing with Openstack look OK and I did not see any change in the heat file recently. But anyway it is not connected to the SDC => issue SO/Openstack
- basic_vm_macro SDC error on check distribution list, reported a empty but pnf-macro, basic_onboard, basic_clamp and basic_network were OK and they use the same mechanism.
3) no regression observed on secu or infra tests

A retry is in progress

Patch Set 8:

the reproducibility of the SO error in basic_vm and basic_vm_macro is weird..
as the patch is dealing with SDC only

it occurs on stack deletion, at the very end of the test (cleanup of the openstack resources)
https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//124233-8/basic_vm/basic_vm/pythonsdk.debug.log

2021-10-07 09:33:52,176 ERROR 148:vf_module_ala_carte.py(1) - VfModule instantiation basic_vm0..BasicVm..base_ubuntu20..module-0 failed
2021-10-07 09:33:52,177 ERROR 48:basic_vm.py(1) - VF Module instantiation error

I assume that the tester needs to be adapted somehow, I do not really see why it comes from a SDC update...but it looks reproducible

So for me patch looks good
but if we merge, we will have regression int the test suite as a side effect

@michal what is your view?
option 1: merge then quick fix on test side
option 2: use a daily, update sdc docker version, test, gate tester, re-gate the patch

Looks like an issue with gating - not with tests or change - I've observed the same issue with other gating runs, like for https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//123761-2/basic_vm/basic_vm/pythonsdk.debug.log. The error is the same: Message: No valid host was found. , Code: 500.
But yesterday some gating runs passed, like: https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating/124660-2/.
I think it's something related with the lab gating and not with the tests or the changes made, because there is one thing all these failed calls have in common: they are made on the same tenant - 311eada36da542acb62af4b2a334c02a
